name: Freezer Mix Chilies Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Dip container of frozen mix in hot water just to loosen.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat frozen mix, water, and enchilada sauce over medium heat until boiling.
3. Reduce heat; cover and simmer, stirring frequently, until the mixture is thawed, about 20 minutes.
4. Heat oven to 350°F.
5. Rinse green and red chilies in cold water to remove seeds (the seeds are very hot). Discard seeds. Cut chilies into small pieces.
6. Arrange 2 cups of corn chips in a greased baking dish, 10x6x1 3/4 inches.
7. Layer half the meat mixture, chili mix, and cheese over the corn chips.
8. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
9. Sprinkle remaining corn chips on top.
10. Bake uncovered until casserole is bubbly, 30 to 35 minutes.
Note: This casserole is hot and spicy. If you prefer a milder flavor, substitute 1 can (2 ounces) sliced pimiento, drained, for the red chilies.
